vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1615: Substrate: work agents hard-wedge at the model context ceiling (400) and are misreported 'healthy'; PAN-1415 remediation can't recover them

plan.title: Substrate: detect, recover, and prevent context-ceiling-wedged work agents
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: A long-running work agent can grow its context past the model's hard ceiling, after which every turn fails with `API Error: 400 Your input exceeds the context window`. The agent is permanently wedged — it cannot accept any new input, including orchestrator messages — yet both health classifiers (dashboard determineHealthStatus and cloister/health.ts) report it 'healthy' because they are purely lastActivity-time based and a wedged agent keeps re-rendering its error. PAN-1415's idle remediation makes it worse: pan tell / resume --message append MORE context to an agent already over the ceiling, so every poke 400s. The existing deacon recovery sends /compact, but /compact must round-trip the full transcript through the model to summarize, so it ALSO 400s at the hard ceiling (Claude Code's own banner recommends /clear). Two gpt-5.5 agents bled ~$73 while wedged, one stuck ~2 days, both reported healthy. For CLIProxy-routed models native auto-compact never fires, so they sail straight into the hard 400.
narratives.Proposal: Three root-cause fixes, no bandaids. (A) DETECT: extract the proven context-overflow tmux-tail predicate into a shared function and have the deacon persist a contextSaturatedAt marker on the agent's runtime.json on first detection (cleared when the tail clears). (B) CLASSIFY: both health classifiers treat a present marker as a distinct non-healthy 'wedged' state that overrides lastActivity 'healthy', so wedged agents are never misreported and operators can see them. (C) RECOVER without appending: add a /clear + reseed-from-continue.json tier to the deacon's existing escalation ladder — /compact first (works under the ceiling), fall back to /clear (frees tokens with no model round-trip) plus a reseed nudge pointing the agent at continue.json/beads/git to reconstruct WIP, and only then markWorkspaceStuck. (D) PREVENT: verify CLIProxy compaction behavior, then add a high-water-mark proactive /compact trigger (~85% percentUsed, idle, under the ceiling, cooldown-guarded) so the agent compacts before it ever 400s. Surface the wedged state in the activity feed and document the new state + recovery ladder.

autoDecisions.summary: Detection signal = the existing tmux-tail CONTEXT_OVERFLOW_PATTERNS match (extracted to a shared predicate), not the heavier server-side percentUsed.
autoDecisions.rationale: The tail match is already proven in checkApiErrorAgents() and is cheap; percentUsed reads the session JSONL per agent and would re-introduce the CPU cost the health route was explicitly optimized to avoid. percentUsed is reserved for the proactive pre-ceiling trigger where a precise number is actually needed.

autoDecisions.summary: Persist the saturation marker on runtime.json (contextSaturatedAt), not as a sticky review-status flag.
autoDecisions.rationale: determineHealthStatus already reads runtime.json cheaply and keys by agentId; a live, self-clearing marker mirrors the current tail and avoids a stale 'wedged forever' state if a human manually recovers the agent. The review-status `stuck` flag remains the terminal escalation only after recovery gives up.

autoDecisions.summary: Recovery uses /clear+reseed (context-dropping) as the hard-ceiling tier, keeping /compact as the under-ceiling tier.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue and Claude Code's own banner show /compact cannot clear a hard-ceiling overflow (it re-sends the transcript to the model). /clear frees tokens with no model round-trip; the workspace files survive a /clear so WIP is reconstructable from continue.json/beads/git. This is the only remediation that does NOT add context to an already-over-ceiling agent.

autoDecisions.summary: Proactive compaction (Expected #3) is included but split into a verify bead that informs a trigger bead, ordered after detection+recovery.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue phrases #3 as 'verify the CLIProxy path also compacts' — the actual ceiling/auto-compact behavior must be established before a threshold trigger is safe to write. The detection+recovery safety net is the higher-value, lower-risk work and lands first.

autoDecisions.summary: No model is blocklisted or special-cased; gpt-5.5 remains a preferred work model.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue and project memory are explicit that this is a substrate gap, not a gpt-5.5 problem. All fixes apply uniformly to CLIProxy-routed and native sessions.

items.title: Extract a shared context-overflow tail predicate with unit tests

narrative.Action: Extract the inline context-overflow detection from src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts checkApiErrorAgents() into a single reusable predicate (e.g. isContextOverflowTail(tail: string): boolean) exported from a shared module, preserving the existing behavior exactly: judge only the recent tail (last ~40 lines) against CONTEXT_OVERFLOW_PATTERNS. Refactor checkApiErrorAgents() to call the new predicate (no behavior change there). This is the single canonical detection contract that the marker, the classifiers, and the recovery tier all consume.

subItems.title: A pure exported predicate (e.g. isContextOverflowTail) returns true for a tail containing 'input exceeds the context window' / 'exceeds the context window of this model' in the last ~40 lines and false otherwise
checkApiErrorAgents() is refactored to call the predicate and its existing overflow-handling behavior is unchanged (existing deacon tests still pass)
Unit tests cover a true hard-wedge tail, a benign tail that merely mentions 'context window' outside the recent window, and an empty tail

items.title: Persist a self-clearing contextSaturatedAt marker on the agent runtime.json

narrative.Action: Add a contextSaturatedAt?: string field to the agent runtime state (AgentRuntimeState in src/lib/agents.ts and the runtime.json persistence path) and helpers to set/clear it. In the deacon patrol's context-overflow handling, when isContextOverflowTail(tail) is true, set contextSaturatedAt on FIRST detection (idempotent — keep the original timestamp on subsequent cycles); when the tail no longer shows overflow, clear it. The marker is a live mirror of the current tail, NOT a sticky flag. This crystallizes the schema both classifiers read.

subItems.title: AgentRuntimeState carries an optional contextSaturatedAt timestamp persisted to and read from runtime.json
The deacon sets contextSaturatedAt the first cycle overflow is detected (preserving the original timestamp across repeated detections) and clears it the first cycle the tail no longer overflows
Writing/clearing the marker does not clobber other runtime.json fields (lastActivity, claudeSessionId, etc.) — verified by a test that round-trips a populated runtime state

items.title: Dashboard /api/health/agents reports a wedged agent as non-healthy

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/lib/health-filtering.ts determineHealthStatus(), when the agent's runtime.json carries contextSaturatedAt, return a distinct non-healthy status (e.g. { status: 'wedged', reason: 'Context window exhausted (400) — recovery in progress' }) that OVERRIDES the lastActivity-based 'healthy'/'warning' path. Read the marker from the runtime.json already loaded for lastActivity — do NOT add a per-agent tmux capture (the route is performance-sensitive). Ensure the health-status type/enum and any consumers tolerate the new value.

subItems.title: An alive agent with a recent lastActivity AND contextSaturatedAt set returns a non-healthy 'wedged' status, not 'healthy'
An alive agent without the marker still returns 'healthy'/'warning'/'stuck' exactly as before (no regression)
No new per-agent tmux/capturePane call is introduced in the /api/health/agents path; the marker is read from the runtime.json already loaded

items.title: Cloister health evaluator surfaces the wedged state in summary and attention checks

narrative.Action: Make src/lib/cloister/health.ts aware of context saturation so the cloister-side view (getAgentHealth, generateHealthSummary, needsAttention) does not report a wedged agent as 'active'. Expose the contextSaturatedAt marker to the evaluator (extend the runtime accessor it already uses, or read the marker alongside the heartbeat) and surface a distinct wedged state in AgentHealth so the health summary counts it and needsAttention() returns true. Do not route wedged agents into the idle stuck-remediation ladder — context recovery is owned by the deacon's overflow path.

subItems.title: getAgentHealth returns a distinct wedged/non-active state when contextSaturatedAt is present, even if the heartbeat is recent
needsAttention() returns true for a wedged agent and generateHealthSummary counts it as non-active
A non-saturated agent's health classification is unchanged (existing cloister health tests pass)

items.title: Pure builder for the post-/clear reseed nudge

narrative.Action: Add a pure function that builds the reseed nudge text sent after a /clear. The message must instruct the agent to reconstruct its work-in-progress WITHOUT any prior transcript: read .pan/continue.json, run `bd ready`/`bd show <id>` for its open beads, inspect `git status`/`git diff` for uncommitted work on disk (which survives /clear), and continue from there — explicitly NOT start over and NOT expect the prior conversation. Keep it transcript-free by construction (it references durable artifacts, never prior messages).

subItems.title: A pure function returns a reseed message that names continue.json, beads (bd ready/bd show), and git status/diff as the reconstruction sources
The message explicitly tells the agent NOT to start over and that its prior conversation was cleared to recover from a context-window overflow
A unit test asserts the message contains the durable-artifact references and the do-not-restart instruction

items.title: Add the /clear + reseed recovery tier to the deacon escalation ladder

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts checkApiErrorAgents(), extend the existing context-overflow escalation so it no longer dead-ends at /compact. New ladder: (1) /compact as today (works while under the ceiling); (2) if after CONTEXT_COMPACT_SETTLE_MS the overflow is STILL present (isContextOverflowTail true), send /clear followed by the reseed-message nudge — never re-sending the transcript; track clear attempts separately in contextOverflowRecoveryState; (3) if the overflow persists after the /clear tier's own attempt budget, markWorkspaceStuck(issueId, 'context_overflow', ...) as the terminal state. Preserve the existing settle-window, cooldown, and stuck/deaconIgnored guards. Emit activity-feed events for the /clear tier as for /compact.

subItems.title: When /compact fails to clear the overflow after the settle window, the deacon sends /clear and then the reseed nudge (in that order) instead of only retrying /compact
The /clear tier never sends the prior transcript or any 'continue' message that appends context before the /clear has freed tokens
If the overflow persists after the /clear+reseed attempt budget is exhausted, the workspace is marked stuck with reason 'context_overflow' and the recovery state is cleared
Existing guards (settle window, recovery cooldown, stuck/deaconIgnored skip) and the /compact-while-under-ceiling path are preserved; tests cover the compact→clear→stuck progression

items.title: Verify CLIProxy auto-compaction behavior and establish a conservative ceiling constant

narrative.Action: Establish, from code and the CLIProxy/model-capabilities path, (a) whether Claude Code's native auto-compact fires for CLIProxy-routed models (e.g. gpt-5.5) or whether — as deacon.ts comments assert — it never fires because the proxied model's context window is undeterminable, and (b) the real hard ceiling the proxy/backend enforces. Deliver the finding as code: a documented conservative ceiling constant (and/or correction to the model-capabilities contextWindow for proxied models) plus an inline comment citing the evidence, consumed by the proactive trigger bead. Record the conclusion in the PR description.

subItems.title: The PR/commit documents whether CLIProxy-routed models auto-compact and what their effective hard ceiling is, with file:line evidence
A documented conservative ceiling constant (or corrected model-capabilities value) is committed for the proactive trigger to consume

items.title: High-water-mark proactive /compact before the hard ceiling

narrative.Action: Add a proactive compaction trigger to the deacon patrol: when an agent's server-side context usage (percentUsed from the conversation-service computation, against the conservative ceiling from proactive-verify) crosses a high-water mark (~85%) AND the agent is idle/at-prompt AND still under the hard ceiling (no overflow yet), send /compact to pre-empt the 400. Cooldown-guarded per agent so a wrong threshold cannot spam /compact, and a no-op once an overflow is already present (the recovery ladder owns that case). This makes the wedge preventable, not just recoverable.

subItems.title: When percentUsed crosses the high-water mark and the agent is idle and not yet overflowing, the deacon sends a single /compact (cooldown-guarded)
The proactive trigger is a no-op when the agent is already overflowing (recovery ladder owns that) and when within the cooldown window
Tests cover: crosses-threshold → compact, below-threshold → no-op, already-overflowing → no-op, within-cooldown → no-op

items.title: Surface the wedged state in the activity feed and document the new health state + recovery ladder

narrative.Action: Emit a clear activity-feed event the first time an agent is marked context-saturated (so operators see the wedge instead of a silent 'healthy'), and document the new 'wedged' health state plus the compact→clear→stuck recovery ladder and the proactive trigger in the relevant deacon/agent-health doc. Keep the activity event idempotent per incident (one event per detection, not per cycle).

subItems.title: An activity-feed event is emitted when contextSaturatedAt is first set for an agent, naming the agent/issue and the context-overflow cause, and is not re-emitted every patrol cycle
Documentation describes the wedged health state, the compact→clear→stuck recovery ladder, and the proactive trigger
